results.title: Free intraabdominal catheter management post-VP shunt disconnection in pediatric patients: systematic review.
results.snippet:
PubMed ID: 40694115. 2025/07/22 00:00. BACKGROUND: Increased intra-abdominal pressure has been reported to result in 
raised intracranial pressure in a variety of conditions such as obesity and 
pregnancy, and it also constitutes an infrequent cause of ventriculoperitoneal 
(VP) shunt malfunction. Patients with neurological deficits, as those with 
myelomeningocele or cerebral palsy, are prone to developing a neurogenic bowel 
and to suffer chronic constipation. Although previously recognized, V
